role_title: Strategic Initiatives & Corporate Affairs Lead, Office of the CEO

location.office_time_required: Denver, CO preferred; US Remote also available

level_signals: 5–8 years of experience in strategy, operations, corporate affairs, or related roles
Reports to VP, Office of the CEO & Corporate Affairs — not a people manager role

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5–8 years of experience in strategy, operations, corporate affairs, or related roles within a high-growth SaaS or technology company.
Experience working on cross-functional projects and supporting initiatives that span multiple teams.
Strong communication skills, with experience developing content for senior leaders or executive audiences.
Understanding of B2B technology businesses and how companies position and operate in competitive markets.
Ability to work effectively with senior stakeholders and navigate ambiguity.
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities at once.
High level of discretion and professionalism in handling sensitive information.
Willingness to learn, take ownership, and operate in a fast-paced environment.
Willingness to travel up to 10% of the time.

scope.surface: Office of the CEO — strategy, corporate affairs, executive effectiveness, cross-functional initiative execution
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS — integration and automation platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Clarity and consistency of company narrative externally and internally
Progress and completion of high-priority cross-functional initiatives
cross_functional_partners: VP Office of the CEO & Corporate Affairs
core_responsibilities: Drive day-to-day coordination across priorities, support executive workflows, and ensure key initiatives, meetings, and follow-ups are well-structured and moving forward.
Help bring Boomi's narrative and positioning to life across key audiences, ensuring clarity and consistency in how we communicate internally and externally.
Lead and support high-priority, cross-functional initiatives on behalf of the CEO and executive team, helping move work forward from concept through execution.
Support how Boomi shows up with customers, partners, and the broader market through content, coordination, and preparation for key moments.
Assist in preparing for critical moments including executive communications, customer and partner engagements, and leadership forums.
Help coordinate leadership cadences and forums to ensure alignment on priorities, decisions, and follow-through.
Operate with a high level of discretion and judgment, effectively representing the Office of the CEO across internal and external interactions.

green_flags: Remote-US eligible with Denver preferred — flexible for most US-based candidates
Explicit acknowledgment of impostor syndrome and encouragement to apply even without perfect fit
High-visibility role reporting directly to VP, Office of the CEO — strong career growth potential
Clear description of responsibilities across multiple core areas
red_flags: No equity mentioned despite being a SaaS company — total compensation picture unclear
Role responsibilities described as 'flex based on business needs' — scope may shift unpredictably
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed anywhere in the JD
Location listed as 'hybrid' inferred — JD says 'Denver, CO (preferred) or US Remote' which is ambiguous about actual hybrid requirements for Denver-based employees

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years required)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description: B2B SaaS integration/automation)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
location.type set to 'hybrid' — JD says 'Denver, CO (preferred) or US Remote'; unclear if Denver presence involves in-office days
ambiguities: Location: JD states 'Denver, CO (preferred) or US Remote' — unclear whether Denver-based employees are expected to be on-site or fully remote. Classified as hybrid due to the city preference signal, but could be interpreted as fully remote-US.

No nice-to-haves are explicitly enumerated in the JD — all listed requirements appear under a single 'The Experience You Bring' section without preferred/required differentiation beyond the implied must-have framing.
